support.summary: No irreplaceable CEO action at this early evaluation stage.
support.detail: ASYAD is early discovery/demo (just moved to Formal Evaluation on 29 Jun, confidence 10%, no compelling event, close 31 Mar 2027). There is no blocked discount, roadmap commitment, SE/POC guarantee, or CEO-to-buyer peer relationship the deal turns on right now. The ROI case, SAP-consolidation mapping, and Oman-hosting validation are standard AE/SE/VP work; CEO air-cover is not yet warranted.
support.why_not_vp: Nothing requires CEO authority; RSD/SE own the ROI build, stakeholder mapping and hosting validation at this stage.

monitor.reason: We owe a case study that is 25 days overdue while our only champion goes cold on a $300k deal.

triggers.summary: Promised Hapag Lloyd case study 25 days overdue as champion goes cold
triggers.detail: On 4 Jun we committed to send meeting slides and the Hapag Lloyd case study to developing champion Jamila Al Bukhaly (due 8 Jun); it remains unsent 25 days later and is not blocked on the buyer. Internal tasks logged 29 Jun flag '10 days without email outbound' and 'Prospect's gone cold - no inbound in 14 days'. On this $300k Formal-Evaluation deal our own follow-through lapse is starving the single champion who initiated after Gartner and owns the RFP, handing default momentum to incumbent SAP Ariba's SAP-ecosystem consolidation pitch.
triggers.metric: Case study 25 days overdue (due 8 Jun); 10+ days no outbound email
triggers.ceo_ask: Ask Mohamad why the Hapag Lloyd case study promised to Jamila on 4 Jun is still unsent 25 days later, and what he is doing this week to re-warm her before Ariba consolidation wins by default.
triggers.evidence: Open deliverable 'Share meeting slides and Hapag Lloyd case study to Jamila Al Bukhaly' status overdue; internal task 29 Jun '10 days without email outbound. Send follow-ups' and 'Prospect's gone cold? No inbound since last 14 days'.
